{{Quote|This is the intergalactic bulk-freighter, ''Nova Prince.'' You and everyone ''else'' down here have been ''impressed'' into service—since no sentient in their right ''mind'' would intentionally sign on for a cruise to the ''Red Nebula''.|A [[mobile hold-tender]]|<ref name="marvel50" />}}
The '''''Nova Prince''''' was a [[Bulk freighter/Legends|bulk freighter]] operated by a group of [[Red Nebula exiles|exiles]] from the [[Red Nebula/Legends|Red Nebula]] during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], the leader of these exiles, [[Klysk]], kept a number of [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]], [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] and [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] [[Prison/Legends|prisoner]] aboard the ''Nova Prince''—these beings were kidnapped on fringe [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] like [[Terminus/Legends|Terminus]], in order to serve as thieves for an attempted heist of the Red Nebula's [[Great Life Jewels]].<ref name="marvel50" />

The exiles were forced to rely on captured [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] to guide the ''Nova Prince'' through an [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] [[Asteroid field/Legends|field]] on the way to the Red Nebula, as the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] was large and not easily maneuverable. Solo and his partner [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] seemingly returned with the jewels not long after, prompting Klysk—who was hoping to bring about the destruction of the Red Nebula via the jewels' destruction—to fly the ''Nova Prince'' into the [[Sun of the Planet of the Red Nebula|star]] at the center of the nebula. However, Solo and Chewbacca had actually hidden the real jewels away, meaning the deaths of the people aboard the ''Nova Prince'' were in vain.<ref name="marvel50" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Nova Prince'' appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 50|''Star Wars 50: The Crimson Forever'']], written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and released in [[1981]].<ref name="marvel50" />
